title: Book I, Chapter 27; Definition I.D19a; Theorem I.T05
affected: Book I, Chapter 27; Definition I.D19a; Theorem I.T05
summary: Corrects the published prime-polarity definition. The original growth-comparison clauses do not partition the primes; the corrected classifier uses the Legendre symbol (2/p), aligning Book I with the Book III bipolar classifier.
correction: Replace Definition I.D19a and the proof of I.T05 with the Legendre-symbol polarity definition and the corresponding partition theorem. Treat the former growth-rate discussion as heuristic motivation, not as the theorem's classifier.

title: Book III, Chapter 18; Definition III.D23; Theorem III.T13
affected: Book III, Chapter 18; Definition III.D23; Theorem III.T13
summary: Corrects the Jacobi-symbol convention used in the internal bipolar classifier derivation. Under the classical Jacobi convention, zero factors do not reduce to a single Legendre factor.
correction: Introduce an explicit spectral Legendre convention for the CRT-local readout. The classifier and label-convergence conclusion remain unchanged; the derivation is reformulated.

title: Book I, Chapter 24; Theorem I.T04
summary: Tightens the Hyperfactorization Theorem statement. Conditions (i)-(iii) alone do not uniquely specify the ABCD tuple; maximal tetration height must be part of the admissibility condition.
correction: Add the maximality clause already used by the greedy-peel construction and uniqueness proof. The theorem's intended canonical decomposition remains unchanged.

title: Panta Rhei Conspectus v1.1 PDF; TauLib trust-budget disclosure
affected: Panta Rhei Conspectus v1.1 PDF; TauLib trust-budget disclosure
summary: Corrects the Conspectus PDF's pre-refactor TauLib trust-budget language. The downloadable v1.1 PDF still describes the interim peer-review state; the pinned public TauLib release has since landed the refactor.
correction: Read the current trust budget as 3 custom axioms and 0 sorry at the pinned TauLib release, with the former Book VII methodological markers represented as inspectable Commitment definitions. The Release Manifest is authoritative for current machine-checkable counts.
